lines: A man is hammered to the chapel cross behind the altar. Cold window light pins him in place.
He is still the size of a man. That is the part your eye keeps returning to.

effects.log: You step back and leave him on the cross. His whisper follows you across the stone.
You cut him loose. The body tears off the spikes and drops to the stone, and the whispering stops.

lines: The ribs have been split down the breastbone and spread into two bone wings. A wet cavity works behind them, slow as a lung.
Thin tendrils have rooted in the cut places. A second mouth has torn through the belly, ringed with bone teeth.
The head has gone over into a goat skull, one horn curled, one horn snapped. Black-gold lines sit under the skin like wire. Nothing spills free.

effects.log: You step back and leave him on the cross. His whisper follows you across the stone.
You cut him loose. The body tears off the spikes and drops to the stone. The whispering stops.

lines: Scraps of a settlement guard's coat are still belted to him. The patched black wool matches the dead at the intake.
The cult did not haul him from the road. He held this house before they made him its warning.

effects.log: You step back and leave him on the cross. His whisper follows you across the stone.
You cut him loose. The body tears off the spikes and drops to the stone. The whispering stops.

lines: His head turns as far as the spikes allow.
"Looking down the cross," he whispers. "Speak no evil."
He says it again, softer. The words are the last rule he can still keep.

effects.log: You step back and leave him on the cross. His whisper follows you across the stone.
You cut him loose. The body tears off the spikes and drops to the stone. The whispering stops.

lines: The body tears free of the spikes. It hits the stone badly, then lies still.
Mercy, evidence destroyed, one less saint for the cult to finish. Censure will name it later. The man cannot.
You leave him hung and half-living. The ribs keep working. The mouth in his belly keeps tasting the chapel air.
Perhaps the Compact would pay to take him alive. Perhaps the Remnant would burn the whole chapel for less. He is still whispering when you turn away.
